summary: Swag Chat iteration 5 — Status (Stories) feature verified end-to-end. BACKEND: 79/79 pytest cases PASS in 94.32s (16 new + 63 existing regression; 1 LLM-flake test deselected per request via -k 'not test_submit_returns'). Added /app/backend/tests/test_status.py with 16 cases covering: (1) POST /api/status type=text returns 200 with id/type/background/viewers=[]/expires_at and heavy fields stripped; (2) text missing → 400; (3) text >700 → 400; (4) type=image with base64 → 200 (caption preserved); (5) image missing → 400; (6) type=video with base64 + duration_ms → 200; (7) video missing → 400; (8) invalid type → 400; (9) GET /api/status/feed as alice shows is_self=true entry with has_unseen=False and statuses[] sorted oldest-first; (10) GET /api/status/feed as bob (alice's friend) shows alice entry with has_unseen=True and seen=False; (11) POST /api/status/{id}/view as bob → feed now shows seen=True; (12) GET /api/status/{id}/viewers as non-author → 403; (13) GET /api/status/{id}/viewers as author returns viewers including bob_test; (14) DELETE /api/status/{id} by non-author → 403; (15) DELETE by author returns {deleted:true} and subsequent GET → 404; (16) GET /api/status/{id} as a fresh non-friend charlie → 403. FRONTEND (390x844 mobile viewport, EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL preview): login as alice@test.com → /chats. StatusStrip renders at the top with testID='status-my-tile' (Avatar shows 'ME' + add-badge before any status). Tapping status-my-tile navigates to /status/compose; the composer screen exposes ALL expected testIDs: status-compose-screen, status-compose-back, status-compose-publish, status-mode-text/image/video, status-text-input, and all 8 background swatches status-bg-#0F172A / #7C3AED / #DC2626 / #059669 / #F59E0B / #0EA5E9 / #9333EA / #1F2937. Typing 'Hello from alice (mobile UI test)' into status-text-input, tapping status-bg-#1F2937, and pressing status-compose-publish successfully created the status (verified via backend log POST /api/status 200 OK) and routed back to /chats; the My-status tile avatar then re-rendered with the user's initials ('AL') instead of 'ME', confirming the feed refreshed on focus. No red screens, no API errors. Pre-existing RNW shadow* and useNativeDriver warnings persist (non-blocking carryover from iters 1-4).

minor.issue: Pre-existing flake (not iter-5): TestVerification::test_submit_returns_structured_response asserts r2.status_code<500 before its 502-fallback branch, so when the upstream LLM gateway returns Cloudflare 502 the test fails before the elif. Deselected for this run via -k 'not test_submit_returns'. Carried over from iteration 4.

react_native_issues.issue: RNW deprecation warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Carryover from iterations 1-4. Reference: multiple StyleSheet.create blocks use shadowColor/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius/shadowOffset (visible on auth + chats screens). Recommend Platform.OS==='web' boxShadow guard.

Carryover warning: 'Animated: useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' Reference: /app/frontend/app/auth.tsx and any FadeIn/reanimated entry animations on web. Non-blocking on web preview.

RNW deprecation: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents.' Emitted once during login submit. Reference: likely a Pressable/View wrapper still passing pointerEvents as a prop instead of as a style. Non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional, carryover from iter 4) Loosen TestVerification::test_submit_returns_structured_response so it asserts 'r2.status_code in (200, 502)' first, then branches — current strict assertion still fails when the upstream LLM gateway returns Cloudflare 502.
(Optional, carryover) Replace shadow* style props with Platform.OS==='web' boxShadow guard to silence RNW warnings.
(Optional, carryover) Disable useNativeDriver on web (Platform.OS==='web') in remaining Animated.* / reanimated entry animations.
(Optional, new) Move 'pointerEvents' from props to style on whichever View/Pressable triggers it during login submit.

(Nice-to-have) /api/status/feed currently always sorts entries with self-first; the items[].statuses[] are 'reverse()'-d so oldest plays first — consistent with spec. Consider documenting this ordering contract inline.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/status.py create_status: WS push at line 70-75 only sends a small {type:'status:new', by, status_id} envelope (does NOT include the heavy base64) — good, no WS frame bloat.
/app/backend/routes/status.py status_feed: heavy fields are excluded at projection time (image_base64:0, video_base64:0). Excellent — feed response stays small.
/app/backend/routes/status.py status_feed: sort key uses created_at parsed via datetime.fromisoformat with a Z-handling shim. Works, but is repeated; consider a tiny helper.
/app/backend/routes/status.py get_status: TTL defensive check is correct (Mongo TTL is eventually-consistent so a manual 410 protects against stragglers).

/app/backend/routes/status.py get_status: returns the FULL doc including image_base64/video_base64 — this is intentional for the viewer screen (per spec). Note for future: payload can be large; consider streaming or a /api/status/{id}/media endpoint if usage grows.
/app/backend/routes/status.py mark_viewed: idempotent ($addToSet), self-view is a no-op (returns viewed:true without persisting). Good.
/app/backend/routes/status.py mark_viewed: 'message:viewed' (here: 'status:viewed') WS is only fired the FIRST time bob views (not the early-return path). Correct.

/app/backend/routes/status.py delete_status: hard delete (delete_one). Per WhatsApp parity this is fine, but consider soft-delete + tombstone WS event so other devices/viewers immediately remove the bubble.
/app/backend/models.py StatusIn allows duration_ms for ALL types — only used when type==video (server filters). Acceptable, no spec impact.

/app/frontend/src/components/StatusStrip.tsx Avatar fallback for 'My status' uses username 'me' before any status exists (renders 'ME'), then switches to alice_test once myItem is populated — verified live. Consider passing the AUTH user's real username to the placeholder so the tile shows correct initials immediately on first paint (small UX polish).
/app/frontend/app/status/compose.tsx publish flow uses router.back(); chats.tsx must therefore refresh /status/feed on focus — verified working (My-tile flipped from 'ME' to 'AL' after publish).

/app/frontend/app/status/compose.tsx The image/video tabs auto-trigger the picker on tap — good UX. Note: on web ImagePicker.launchImageLibraryAsync uses an <input type=file> which won't open in headless Playwright (not blocking for this test, but worth noting for future automation).

success_rate.backend: 100% (79/79 pytest cases passed; 1 pre-existing LLM-gateway flake intentionally deselected per request). 16/16 NEW iteration-5 status tests passed.
success_rate.frontend: 100% (alice login → /chats → status-my-tile visible (shows 'ME' before publish) → tap → /status/compose → status-compose-screen + all 3 mode tabs + status-text-input + 8 status-bg-#xxxxxx swatches + status-compose-publish all present → publish → return to /chats → My-tile re-rendered with user initials 'AL' confirming feed refresh).

seed_data_creation: No new seeded users. The new /app/backend/tests/test_status.py reuses alice/bob from conftest.py and re-establishes the alice ↔ bob friendship idempotently via /api/users/search?username=bob_test → /api/requests → /api/requests/{id}/respond. For the non-friend 403 case it signs up a fresh charlie_<random>@test.com, then flips users.verified=true directly via pymongo (sync, avoids the closed-loop issue with motor) so the status endpoint can be exercised. Test data is cleaned up via DELETE /api/status/{id} both per-test and in module teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL: https://face-verify-chat-2.preview.emergentagent.com (EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). Run: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/ -v --tb=line -k 'not test_submit_returns' --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iteration_N.xml. New status tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_status.py (16 tests, all green). Mobile viewport for UI runs: 390x844. Test creds: alice@test.com/Test@1234, bob@test.com/Test@1234 (/app/memory/test_credentials.md). Key status endpoints: POST /api/status, GET /api/status/feed, GET /api/status/{id}, POST /api/status/{id}/view, GET /api/status/{id}/viewers (author only), DELETE /api/status/{id} (author only). WS events emitted: 'status:new' (small envelope to friends), 'status:viewed' (to author when a friend views). Frontend testIDs (chats screen): status-my-tile; (compose screen): status-compose-screen, status-compose-back, status-compose-publish, status-mode-text, status-mode-image, status-mode-video, status-text-input, status-bg-#0F172A / #7C3AED / #DC2626 / #059669 / #F59E0B / #0EA5E9 / #9333EA / #1F2937. Login testIDs: auth-email-input, auth-password-input, auth-password-toggle, auth-submit-button. NOTE: the Auth screen has TWO 'Log In' tabs vs button — click the testID 'auth-submit-button', not the role-based button locator (the tab also matches role=button name='Log In'). The non-friend test creates a fresh charlie_<rand>@test.com user and flips verified=true via pymongo because motor's async loop is already closed by pytest fixtures.
rca_of_issue: No new bugs found in iteration 5. Pre-existing carryover: (a) /api/verification/submit LLM-gateway 502 flake — test asserts <500 before its 502 elif (test-logic bug, not product); (b) RNW shadow*/useNativeDriver/pointerEvents deprecation warnings on web — cosmetic, non-blocking. During UI test the first attempt to click a role-based 'Log In' button matched the auth-tab toggle (two elements share text 'Log In'); fixed by using the testID 'auth-submit-button' (or page.get_by_text('Log In').nth(-1)). Documented in context_for_next_testing_agent.
